text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is an open book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Strictly observe the word limit of 1125 words per essay.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: Discuss how macrophages participate in the control of and recovery from infection by different pathogens.
Describe how allo-antigens are presented and recognised by the host immune system after solid organ transplantation
Compare the replication strategies of influenza virus, herpesviruses and HIV. Comment on how anti-viral drugs inhibit the replication of these three viruses.
Discuss how viruses modify the infected cell and how these modifications benefit the virus.
How do bacteria use protein toxins and effectors to manipulate the eukaryotic cytoskeleton to promote their colonization, survival and dissemination in the host?
Compare and contrast anaemias caused by impaired generation of red blood cells or their constituents versus those caused by increased destruction of red blood cells.
Discuss cancer development as an evolutionary process and how this is driven by environmental factors and genetic changes.
Compare mechanisms of immunopathology associated with severe disease in human malaria (Plasmodium falciparum) and schistosomiasis (Schistosoma mansoni).
